quiz.title: Unit 9 Quiz 9, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Purity, Mass-Mass Stoichiometry, and Gas Volumes
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Percentage Purity, Mass of Pure Substance from Impure Samples, Mass-Mass Stoichiometry, Gas Volumes at STP, Mole Ratios

questions.question: What mass of sodium chloride is produced when 5.85 g of sodium reacts with excess chlorine gas?
questions.explanation:
2Na + Cl₂ → 2NaCl
Molar mass Na = 23 g/mol, NaCl = 58.44 g/mol
Moles Na = 5.85/23 = 0.254 mol
Mole ratio Na:NaCl = 2:2 = 1:1
Moles NaCl = 0.254 mol
Mass NaCl = 0.254 × 58.44 = 14.8 g

questions.question: How many grams of precipitate are formed when 10.6 g of sodium sulfate reacts with excess barium chloride?
questions.explanation:
Na₂SO₄ + BaCl₂ → BaSO₄↓ + 2NaCl
Molar mass Na₂SO₄ = 142.04 g/mol, BaSO₄ = 233.39 g/mol
Moles Na₂SO₄ = 10.6/142.04 = 0.0746 mol
Mole ratio Na₂SO₄:BaSO₄ = 1:1
Moles BaSO₄ = 0.0746 mol
Mass BaSO₄ = 0.0746 × 233.39 = 17.4 g

questions.question: Calculate the percentage purity of a 9.0 g calcium carbonate sample containing 7.2 g of pure calcium carbonate.
questions.explanation:
Percentage purity = (mass of pure substance / total mass) × 100%
= (7.2/9.0) × 100% = 80.0%

questions.question: How many moles of nitrogen monoxide are formed when 14.0 mol of nitrogen reacts with excess oxygen?
questions.explanation:
N₂ + O₂ → 2NO
Mole ratio N₂:NO = 1:2
Moles NO = 14.0 × 2 = 28.0 mol

questions.question: What mass of aluminum oxide is produced when 13.5 g of aluminum reacts completely with oxygen?
questions.explanation:
4Al + 3O₂ → 2Al₂O₃
Molar mass Al = 27 g/mol, Al₂O₃ = 102.0 g/mol
Moles Al = 13.5/27 = 0.50 mol
Mole ratio Al:Al₂O₃ = 4:2 = 2:1
Moles Al₂O₃ = 0.50/2 = 0.25 mol
Mass Al₂O₃ = 0.25 × 102.0 = 25.5 g

questions.question: A 20.0 g sample of sodium chloride is only 95% pure. How many grams of pure sodium chloride does it contain?
How many moles of carbon monoxide are produced when 8.0 mol of carbon reacts with limited oxygen?
questions.explanation:
2C + O₂ → 2CO
Mole ratio C:CO = 2:2 = 1:1
Moles CO = 8.0 mol (assuming sufficient O₂ for this amount)

questions.question: What mass of calcium hydroxide is formed when 7.4 g of calcium oxide reacts with excess water?
questions.explanation:
CaO + H₂O → Ca(OH)₂
Molar mass CaO = 56.08 g/mol, Ca(OH)₂ = 74.10 g/mol
Moles CaO = 7.4/56.08 = 0.132 mol
Mole ratio CaO:Ca(OH)₂ = 1:1
Moles Ca(OH)₂ = 0.132 mol
Mass Ca(OH)₂ = 0.132 × 74.10 = 9.78 g ≈ 9.8 g

questions.question: Calculate the percentage purity of a 12.0 g magnesium sample that contains 10.2 g of pure magnesium.
How many grams of lead(II) chloride precipitate are formed when 8.3 g of lead(II) nitrate reacts with excess sodium chloride?
questions.explanation:
Pb(NO₃)₂ + 2NaCl → PbCl₂↓ + 2NaNO₃
Molar mass Pb(NO₃)₂ = 331.2 g/mol, PbCl₂ = 278.1 g/mol
Moles Pb(NO₃)₂ = 8.3/331.2 = 0.0251 mol
Mole ratio Pb(NO₃)₂:PbCl₂ = 1:1
Moles PbCl₂ = 0.0251 mol
Mass PbCl₂ = 0.0251 × 278.1 = 6.98 g

questions.question: How many moles of ammonia are formed when 4.0 mol of nitrogen reacts with excess hydrogen?
A fertilizer sample has a mass of 25.0 g and contains 18.5 g of pure compound. What is its percentage purity?
What mass of silver chloride precipitate forms when 6.8 g of silver nitrate reacts with excess sodium chloride?
questions.explanation:
AgNO₃ + NaCl → AgCl↓ + NaNO₃
Molar mass AgNO₃ = 169.87 g/mol, AgCl = 143.32 g/mol
Moles AgNO₃ = 6.8/169.87 = 0.0400 mol
Mole ratio AgNO₃:AgCl = 1:1
Moles AgCl = 0.0400 mol
Mass AgCl = 0.0400 × 143.32 = 5.73 g

questions.question: A 14.0 g impure sample of potassium nitrate contains 11.9 g of pure compound. Calculate the percentage purity.
questions.explanation:
Percentage purity = (mass of pure substance / total mass) × 100%
= (11.9 / 14.0) × 100% = 0.85 × 100% = 85.0%

questions.question: How many moles of calcium carbonate are required to produce 11.2 L of carbon dioxide at STP?
questions.explanation:
CaCO₃ → CaO + CO₂
At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles CO₂ = 11.2/22.4 = 0.50 mol
Mole ratio CaCO₃:CO₂ = 1:1
Moles CaCO₃ = 0.50 mol

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered purity calculations and stoichiometry! 🧪⚖️
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review percentage purity calculations and mole ratios.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on converting mass to moles correctly and identifying limiting reactants.
scoreMessages.0: Please review stoichiometric calculations, purity concepts, and mole ratio applications.
